hero.question: Will One Battle After Another win Best Cinematography at the 98th Academy Awards?
hero.prob_take: Smart money is piling in on One Battle After Another's cinematography odds, likely reacting to guild precursor wins or behind-the-scenes momentum that retail hasn't fully priced in yet. At 75.5%, the market is already pricing this as a heavy favorite, which leaves little room for error if the film drops a single major precursor or if Academy voters split their attention across a deeper field than expected.

daily_take.headline: Cinematography odds surge 3 points. Is the market pricing in a lock or a trap?
daily_take.deck: One Battle After Another just jumped to 75.5% for Best Cinematography, a sign that either the film's technical case has solidified or the market is front-running consensus. The real question: at this price, you're getting paid 1.3 to 1 for a "lock" that still loses 25% of the time. Sharp bettors should check if recent industry chatter justifies the move or if this is mispricers piling in. The counterplay is waiting for the film to touch 80%, then fading hard.
